name: Butler County Teachers
description_short: Butler County Teachers Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving educators in Pennsylvania with savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans since 1939.
description_long: Butler County Teachers Federal Credit Union was established in 1939 and operates as a federally chartered, NCUA-insured credit union headquartered in Butler, Pennsylvania. With approximately $28.33 million in assets, the institution serves a defined membership base primarily composed of educators and school employees across multiple Pennsylvania school districts and educational institutions. The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), mortgages, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), auto loans, and personal loans. According to the website, they provide competitive rates including a 4.20% APY on savings, 4.59% APY on 1-year CDs, and mortgage rates starting at 4.39% for 15-year terms and 4.91% for 30-year terms as of April 2026. What distinguishes Butler County Teachers is its focused membership model. Unlike open-membership credit unions, membership is restricted to employees of specific Butler County educational institutions including Butler Area School District, Butler Catholic, Butler County Community College, and multiple other named school districts. This specialization allows the credit union to align its services with the needs of educators and school personnel. The institution maintains relatively modest size compared to national banks, which may allow for personalized service within their membership community. However, potential members should note significant limitations. The website also displays California-based competitor rates rather than complete Butler County Teachers product information. Additionally, the $28.33 million in assets is considerably smaller than regional banking alternatives, and membership eligibility restrictions exclude most consumers. The modest asset base and loan portfolio ($8.87 million) suggest limited lending capacity for larger financial needs. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of deposit (CDs) including 1-year, 2-year, and 5-year terms
pros: NCUA federally insured deposits, protecting up to $250,000 per account
Founded in 1939 with 85+ years of operational history serving educators
Offers competitive rates: 4.20% APY savings, 4.59% 1-year CDs, 4.39%-4.91% mortgages
Field of membership focused on educators creates community alignment and tailored services
Comprehensive product suite including savings, CDs, mortgages, HELOCs, auto/personal loans
Member-owned cooperative structure eliminates external shareholder profit extraction
cons: Rate information incomplete on website; states 'We do not yet have rates for Butler County Teachers' and 'no loan rates available'
Restrictive field of membership limits access to employees of specific Pennsylvania school districts only
Very small asset base ($28.33M) and loan portfolio ($8.87M) limits lending capacity for larger loans
Limited branch presence and no online banking platform information provided
Minimal transparency about current product offerings and terms on their profile page
best_for: Employees of Butler Area School District, Butler County Community College, and named Pennsylvania school districts seeking member-owned banking
Educators seeking NCUA-insured savings and modest personal/auto loan needs within their community
School district employees prioritizing credit union member benefits over national bank convenience
diagnosis: Butler County Teachers Federal Credit Union is best for Pennsylvania educators employed by specific school districts seeking member-owned banking with modest rate competitiveness. The primary caveat is severe membership restrictions (school district employees only) and incomplete rate transparency on their profile, making it difficult to assess current product terms and competitiveness compared to alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Butler County Teachers Federal Credit Union serves Pennsylvania educators with savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans since 1939.
